title: lilium canadense subsp. michiganense
intro: Lilium canadense subsp. michiganense, also known as the Michigan lily, is a subspecies of the Canada lily, a perennial plant that belongs to the lily family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant properties. The Michigan lily's health benefits are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
